title: Houses for Rent in the Warehouse District, Cleveland
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Warehouse District, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Searching for houses for rent in the Warehouse District, Cleveland? See what's really available downtown and tell our local team what you're looking for.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent Warehouse District cleveland
answerBox: The Warehouse District is part of Downtown Cleveland's central business district, and its rental housing is almost entirely loft and apartment conversions inside historic 19th-century commercial buildings — true single-family houses for rent are rare to nonexistent here. Renters who want a house typically look at nearby city neighborhoods instead.

sections.h2: Are there houses for rent in the Warehouse District?
body: Honestly, almost never as standalone single-family houses. The Warehouse District sits inside Cleveland's Downtown Statistical Planning Area, between Public Square and the Cuyahoga River, and its historic 19th-century warehouse and commercial buildings along **West 6th Street and St. Clair Avenue** have been converted into loft-style apartments rather than detached houses with yards. If your search for "houses for rent Warehouse District" is really a search for downtown living, this guide explains what the housing stock actually looks like and where the rental homes are.

We work with 90+ Section 8-friendly rental homes across Greater Cleveland, but our current selection is concentrated on the city's East and Southeast sides rather than in the Warehouse District itself. If you tell our local team what you're looking for — including whether a downtown loft or a house in another neighborhood would work — we can point you toward what's realistic and [share what's currently available](/p/book-showing).

sections.h2: What does it cost to rent in Downtown Cleveland?
body: Downtown Cleveland rents run higher than the citywide average because the Warehouse District's converted-loft buildings are professionally managed and often include amenities like elevators, fitness rooms, and secured entry. Per RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report, the average Cleveland-area professionally-managed apartment rents for **$1,564/month** at 787 square feet — and downtown loft buildings typically price at or above that citywide average. Zumper's July 4, 2026 report puts the broader Cleveland median at $1,250/month, which better reflects older, smaller units outside downtown.

sections.h2: Getting around from the Warehouse District
body: The Warehouse District sits next to **Tower City**, the hub where every GCRTA rail line converges — the Red Line (heavy rail to Cleveland Hopkins Airport and the East Side), the Blue and Green Lines to Shaker Heights, and the Waterfront Line to the lakefront attractions near North Coast Harbor. Public Square, the city's central plaza, is a short walk away, and I-90 and the Shoreway provide quick access out of downtown by car.

items: **Location:** Downtown Cleveland, between Public Square and the Cuyahoga River
**Transit hub:** Tower City — Red, Blue, Green, and Waterfront rail lines all connect here
**Nearby landmarks:** Public Square, West 6th Street entertainment corridor, North Coast Harbor
**Housing stock:** Converted 19th-century warehouse and commercial buildings, mostly loft apartments

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers and the Warehouse District
body: The Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA) administers Housing Choice Vouchers for the county, including Downtown Cleveland, and accepts preliminary applications year-round through its [online applicant portal](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php). Whether a specific downtown building accepts a voucher is up to that building's owner — Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept Section 8, and the City of Cleveland itself does not currently have a source-of-income protection ordinance. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, though our voucher-friendly inventory is concentrated outside the Warehouse District. See our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the full picture.

sections.h2: Cleveland neighborhoods with more house inventory
body: If a real house — with a yard, a driveway, or a basement — matters more to you than a downtown address, several Cleveland neighborhoods have a much deeper supply of single-family and small multi-family rental homes. We work with voucher-friendly rental homes in neighborhoods including [Slavic Village](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-slavic-village-cleveland/), [Buckeye](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-buckeye-cleveland/), and [Hough](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-hough-cleveland/), all within a short drive of Downtown. You can also browse our full [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) hub to compare neighborhoods, or [tell us what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) and we'll point you to homes that match.

faq.q: Can I actually rent a house in the Warehouse District?
faq.a: Rarely. The Warehouse District's building stock is almost entirely converted historic warehouses now operated as loft apartments, not detached single-family houses. If you specifically want a house with a yard, nearby Cleveland neighborhoods like Slavic Village, Buckeye, and Hough have far more house-style rental inventory.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent downtown in Cleveland?
faq.a: Per RentCafe's July 2026 report, professionally-managed Cleveland apartments — the closest comparison to Warehouse District lofts — average $1,564/month. Zumper's July 2026 citywide median, which includes older housing stock outside downtown, is lower at $1,250/month.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with homes in the Warehouse District?
faq.a: Our 90+ rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland's East and Southeast side neighborhoods, not the Warehouse District itself. Tell our team what you're looking for and we can point you toward available Section 8-friendly homes in the neighborhoods we do serve.

That depends on each building's owner — Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept Housing Choice Vouchers, and Cleveland does not currently have a source-of-income protection ordinance. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready, though our inventory sits mainly outside downtown.

faq.q: How do I get around without a car from the Warehouse District?
faq.a: The Warehouse District sits right next to Tower City, where GCRTA's Red, Blue, Green, and Waterfront rail lines all converge, making it one of the most transit-connected spots in the region for reaching the airport, University Circle, Shaker Heights, or the lakefront.
